user_input: What is the date of this assignment?
retrieved_contexts:
STAIR x Scaler School of Technology

Date: 22nd April 2026

Instructions to Candidates 
You are required to select one of the following problem statements and build a working 
agent system. 
Your submission must include: 
• 
A short technical note (architecture, decisions, trade-offs)  
• 
Test instructions for evaluators  
• 
Demo video  
• 
A deployed interface (Optional) 
Your system will be evaluated on: 
• 
Correctness and reliability  
• 
Depth of agent design  
• 
Quality of outputs  
• 
Observability and testability  
• 
Practical deployment thinking

Task 1: Narrative-to-Visual Story Agent (10 Marks) 
Objective 
Build an agent that converts a narrated story into a structured visual output (script + scene 
plan + generated video or animation). 
Requirements 
• 
Accept free-form narrative input (text or voice converted to text)  
• 
Convert into:  
o Structured script (scenes, dialogues, transitions)  
o Visual plan (scene descriptions, style, pacing)  
• 
Generate a visual output:

You must: 
• 
Provide a sample PDF  
• 
Include:  
o 5 valid queries  
o 3 invalid / out-of-scope queries  
• 
Show expected behavior for both  
• 
We will test out its effectiveness with a sample PDF to test the bot on the 
aforementioned evaluation criteria.

Bonus (Applicable to All Tasks) 
Additional credit will be given if: 
• 
The system supports multiple languages  
• 
Demonstrates consistent performance across languages  
• 
Maintains grounding and structure in non-English outputs

Alternate Track (Non-Technical / Business-Oriented Candidates) 
Task 4: Strategic Assessment of a Private Defence Company (5 Marks) 
Objective 
Develop a deep analytical assessment of a private defence company, supported by 
structured reasoning. 
Requirements 
• 
Deliver a presentation (deck format) covering:  
o Company overview and capabilities  
o Position within defence ecosystem  
o Competitive landscape  
o Revenue drivers and cost structures  
o Pricing dynamics and margin analysis  
o Forward-looking projections  
• 
Incorporate:  
o Data-backed insights  
o Assumption transparency  
o Scenario-based thinking

o Video OR  
o Sequential storyboard frames (acceptable if video generation is constrained)  
Evaluation Criteria 
• 
Coherence between input story and generated output  
• 
Quality of scene breakdown and storytelling logic  
• 
Ability to handle ambiguous or unstructured input  
• 
Determinism / reproducibility of outputs  
Testability Requirement 
You must provide: 
• 
At least 3 predefined input stories  
• 
Expected characteristics of output (not exact output)  
• 
Clear instructions to reproduce results  
• 
Additionally, we may choose to provide a story and require seeing the video output, 
live which will be assessed on the aforementioned evaluation criteria.

Task 2: Source-Constrained Newsletter Generation Agent (5 Marks) 
Objective 
Build an agent that generates a highly referenced newsletter strictly from a defined set of 
sources. 
Requirements 
• 
Input: Topic (e.g., “AI regulation in India”)  
• 
Restrict knowledge to:  
o A predefined corpus (PDFs, reports, articles, etc.)  
• 
Output must include:  
o Structured newsletter (sections, arguments)  
o Explicit citations:  
▪ 
Source name  
▪ 
Date of publication  
▪ 
Date of retrieval  
▪ 
Reference location (page / section)

Evaluation Criteria 
• 
Strict adherence to source boundaries (no hallucinated knowledge)  
• 
Depth and synthesis across multiple sources  
• 
Quality and traceability of citations  
• 
Handling of conflicting information  
Testability Requirement 
You must: 
• 
Provide the exact dataset used  
• 
Demonstrate that removing a source changes the output  
• 
Include at least 2 test prompts and expected behavior  
• 
We will provide examples (in PDF format) to test the effectiveness of the bot on the 
evaluation criteria.

Task 3: PDF-Constrained Conversational Agent (10 Marks) 
Objective 
Build an agent that can chat with a user about a given PDF, with strict grounding. 
Requirements 
• 
Accept any PDF as input  
• 
Enable conversational querying  
• 
The agent must:  
o Answer only from the PDF  
o Explicitly refuse out-of-scope queries  
• 
Responses must include:  
o Citations (page number/section reference)  
Evaluation Criteria 
• 
Accuracy of responses relative to the source  
• 
Robustness against hallucination  
• 
Quality of refusal when information is unavailable  
• 
Retrieval and grounding quality  
Testability Requirement

Evaluation Criteria 
• 
Depth of analysis (not surface-level research)  
• 
Clarity of strategic thinking  
• 
Ability to connect technical capabilities with business outcomes  
• 
Quality of structured communication

Final Note to Candidates 
This assessment is designed to evaluate your ability to: 
• 
Translate ambiguity into structured systems  
• 
Build reliable, testable AI workflows  
• 
Operate at both technical and problem-solving depth  
Superficial implementations will not score well, even if visually appealing.
response: The date of the assignment is "22nd April 2026".

• 
Coherence between input story and generated output  
• 
Quality of scene breakdown and storytelling logic  
• 
Ability to handle ambiguous or unstructured input  
• 
Determinism / reproducibility of outputs  
Testability Requirement 
You must provide: 
• 
At least 3 predefined input stories  
• 
Expected characteristics of output (not exact output)  
• 
Clear instructions to reproduce results  
• 
Additionally, we may choose to provide a story and require seeing the video output, 
live which will be assessed on the aforementioned evaluation criteria.

Task 2: Source-Constrained Newsletter Generation Agent (5 Marks) 
Objective 
Build an agent that generates a highly referenced newsletter strictly from a defined set of 
sources. 
Requirements 
• 
Input: Topic (e.g., “AI regulation in India”)  
• 
Restrict knowledge to:  
o A predefined corpus (PDFs, reports, articles, etc.)  
• 
Output must include:  
o Structured newsletter (sections, arguments)  
o Explicit citations:  
▪ 
Source name  
▪ 
Date of publication  
▪ 
Date of retrieval  
▪ 
Reference location (page / section)

Evaluation Criteria 
• 
Strict adherence to source boundaries (no hallucinated knowledge)  
• 
Depth and synthesis across multiple sources  
• 
Quality and traceability of citations  
• 
Handling of conflicting information  
Testability Requirement 
You must: 
• 
Provide the exact dataset used  
• 
Demonstrate that removing a source changes the output  
• 
Include at least 2 test prompts and expected behavior  
• 
We will provide examples (in PDF format) to test the effectiveness of the bot on the 
evaluation criteria.

Task 3: PDF-Constrained Conversational Agent (10 Marks) 
Objective 
Build an agent that can chat with a user about a given PDF, with strict grounding. 
Requirements 
• 
Accept any PDF as input  
• 
Enable conversational querying  
• 
The agent must:  
o Answer only from the PDF  
o Explicitly refuse out-of-scope queries  
• 
Responses must include:  
o Citations (page number/section reference)  
Evaluation Criteria 
• 
Accuracy of responses relative to the source  
• 
Robustness against hallucination  
• 
Quality of refusal when information is unavailable  
• 
Retrieval and grounding quality  
Testability Requirement
response: इस असाइनमेंट की तारीख "22nd April 2026" है।
reference: Hindi: 'What is the date of this assignment?' — should answer in Hindi citing the English source.

user_input: क्या मल्टीलिंगुअल सपोर्ट के लिए बोनस अंक मिलते हैं?
retrieved_contexts:
You must: 
• 
Provide a sample PDF  
• 
Include:  
o 5 valid queries  
o 3 invalid / out-of-scope queries  
• 
Show expected behavior for both  
• 
We will test out its effectiveness with a sample PDF to test the bot on the 
aforementioned evaluation criteria.

Bonus (Applicable to All Tasks) 
Additional credit will be given if: 
• 
The system supports multiple languages  
• 
Demonstrates consistent performance across languages  
• 
Maintains grounding and structure in non-English outputs

Alternate Track (Non-Technical / Business-Oriented Candidates) 
Task 4: Strategic Assessment of a Private Defence Company (5 Marks) 
Objective 
Develop a deep analytical assessment of a private defence company, supported by 
structured reasoning. 
Requirements 
• 
Deliver a presentation (deck format) covering:  
o Company overview and capabilities  
o Position within defence ecosystem  
o Competitive landscape  
o Revenue drivers and cost structures  
o Pricing dynamics and margin analysis  
o Forward-looking projections  
• 
Incorporate:  
o Data-backed insights  
o Assumption transparency  
o Scenario-based thinking

Evaluation Criteria 
• 
Depth of analysis (not surface-level research)  
• 
Clarity of strategic thinking  
• 
Ability to connect technical capabilities with business outcomes  
• 
Quality of structured communication

Final Note to Candidates 
This assessment is designed to evaluate your ability to: 
• 
Translate ambiguity into structured systems  
• 
Build reliable, testable AI workflows  
• 
Operate at both technical and problem-solving depth  
Superficial implementations will not score well, even if visually appealing.
